Project N.U.R.S.E
Install the app for a full-screen,
distraction-free study experience
📱 iPhone / iPad — Safari
1 ⬆️ Tap the Share button at the bottom of Safari
2 ➕ Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
3 ✅ Tap Add — then open the app from your home screen icon
⚠️ Switch to Safari
1 📋 Copy the link from the address bar above
2 🧭 Open Safari and paste the link there
3 ⬆️ Tap Share → Add to Home Screen in Safari
🤖 Android — Chrome
1 ⋮ Tap the three-dot menu (⋮) in the top-right of Chrome
2 ➕ Tap "Add to Home screen"
3 ✅ Tap Add — then open the app from your home screen
🖥️ Desktop Browser
1 ⛶ Press F11 (Windows/Linux) or Ctrl+Shift+F (Mac) for full screen
2 ➕ Or click ⋮ menu → Cast, save, and share → Install page as app in Chrome
✅ Once installed, reopen from your home screen icon for full effect
or

🌡️TemperatureAll Ages
ParameterNormal RangeNote
Adult (oral)36.5 – 37.5 °C(97.7–99.5°F)Standard baseline
Adult (rectal)37.0 – 38.1 °C(98.6–100.6°F)+0.5°C vs oral
Adult (axillary)35.9 – 37.0 °C(96.6–98.6°F)−0.5°C vs oral
Neonate36.5 – 37.5 °CRectal preferred
Fever (adult)≥38.0°C / 100.4°FPyrexia
Hyperthermia≥40.5°C / 105°FLife-threatening
Hypothermia<35.0°C / 95°FDanger: cardiac risk
💓Heart Rate (Pulse)
Age GroupNormal BPMFlag
Neonate (0–1mo)100 – 160
Infant (1–12mo)80 – 140
Toddler (1–3y)80 – 130
Preschool (3–6y)80 – 120
School-age (6–12y)70 – 110
Adolescent (12–18y)60 – 100
Adult60 – 100
Tachycardia (adult)>100 bpmAssess cause
Bradycardia (adult)<60 bpmAssess hemodynamics
Critical high>150 bpmNotify MD

Age GroupNormal (breaths/min)

Adolescent/Adult12 – 20
Tachypnea (adult)>20
Bradypnea (adult)<12
Critical<8 or >30

CategorySystolicDiastolic
Normal<120 mmHg<80 mmHg

Hypotension<90 systolicShock risk

ParameterNormalAction Level
SpO₂ (adult)95 – 100%<90% = critical
SpO₂ (neonate)95 – 100%Target 90–95%

Urine output (child)1–2 mL/kg/hr
24-hr urine (adult)800–2000 mL/day
Oliguria<400 mL/dayRenal concern
Anuria<100 mL/dayRenal failure

CategoryResponseScore

None1

No response1

ParameterMaleFemale

⬜White Blood Cell (WBC) & Differential
ParameterNormal RangeSignificance
WBC total5,000–10,000 /µL
Neutrophils (Segs)55–70%Bacterial infection ↑
Bands0–5%Left shift = acute infection
Lymphocytes20–40%Viral infection ↑
Monocytes2–8%Chronic infection
Eosinophils1–4%Allergy/parasite ↑
Basophils0.5–1%Rare; allergy
Leukopenia<5,000Neutropenic precautions
Leukocytosis>10,000Assess for infection
Critical low<2,000Severe immunosuppression
🟣Platelets & Hgb Critical Values
ParameterNormalCritical
Platelets150,000–400,000 /µL<50,000 = bleed risk
Thrombocytopenia<150,000<20,000 = spontaneous bleed
Hgb critical low<7 g/dL → transfuse?
Hgb critical high>20 g/dL → polycythemia
Hct (dehydration)>55%
⚗️ Basic & Comprehensive Metabolic Panel
🧪ElectrolytesCRITICAL NURSE VALUES
ElectrolyteNormalCritical LowCritical High
Sodium (Na⁺)135–145 mEq/L<120>160
Potassium (K⁺)3.5–5.0 mEq/L<2.5>6.5
Chloride (Cl⁻)98–106 mEq/L<80>115
Bicarbonate (HCO₃⁻)22–26 mEq/L<10>40
Calcium (Ca²⁺)8.5–10.5 mg/dL<6.5>13
Magnesium (Mg²⁺)1.5–2.5 mEq/L<1.0>4.5
Phosphorus (PO₄)2.5–4.5 mg/dL<1.0>8.0
🍬Glucose & Renal Function
ParameterNormalFlag
Glucose (fasting)70–100 mg/dL<40 or >500 = critical
Glucose (random)<200 mg/dL
HbA1c (diabetic control)<5.7% normal≥6.5% = DM
BUN8–20 mg/dL>100 = critical
Creatinine (male)0.7–1.3 mg/dL>10 = critical
Creatinine (female)0.6–1.1 mg/dL
BUN:Creatinine ratio10:1 – 20:1>20 = pre-renal
eGFR (normal)≥60 mL/min/1.73m²<15 = kidney failure
Uric acid (male)3.5–7.2 mg/dL
Uric acid (female)2.6–6.0 mg/dL
🫀Liver Function Tests (LFTs)
ParameterNormal Range
ALT (SGPT)7–56 U/L — liver specific
AST (SGOT)10–40 U/L
Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP)44–147 U/L
Total Bilirubin0.1–1.2 mg/dL
Direct Bilirubin0.0–0.3 mg/dL
Albumin3.5–5.0 g/dL
Total Protein6.3–8.2 g/dL
GGT8–61 U/L
💨 Arterial Blood Gases
💨ABG Normal ValuesMEMORIZE
ParameterNormal RangeMnemonic
pH7.35 – 7.45ROME: pH opp. to ↑CO₂
PaCO₂35 – 45 mmHgRespiratory component
HCO₃⁻22 – 26 mEq/LMetabolic component
PaO₂80 – 100 mmHgOxygenation
SaO₂95 – 100%O₂ saturation
Base Excess−2 to +2 mEq/L
📊ABG Interpretation Guide (ROME)
ConditionpHPaCO₂HCO₃⁻
Resp. Acidosis<7.35>45↑Normal/↑ (comp.)
Resp. Alkalosis>7.45<35↓Normal/↓ (comp.)
Met. Acidosis<7.35Normal/↓ (comp.)<22↓
Met. Alkalosis>7.45Normal/↑ (comp.)>26↑
ROME mnemonic: Respiratory Opposite (pH↑, CO₂↓ / pH↓, CO₂↑) — Metabolic Equal (pH↓, HCO₃↓ / pH↑, HCO₃↑)
🎯ABG Step-by-Step Interpretation
Step 1: Check pH → Normal (7.35–7.45), Acidosis (<7.35), Alkalosis (>7.45)
Step 2: Check PaCO₂ → Is it the cause? CO₂↑ with pH↓ = Respiratory Acidosis
Step 3: Check HCO₃⁻ → Is it the cause? HCO₃↓ with pH↓ = Metabolic Acidosis
Step 4: Is there compensation? (other value moving same direction as pH change)
Step 5: Check PaO₂ → Hypoxemia (<80), Severe (<60)
🩹 Coagulation Studies
🩹Coagulation Panel
TestNormalTherapeutic/Critical
PT (Prothrombin Time)11–13.5 sec
INR0.8–1.2Warfarin target: 2–3
aPTT25–35 secHeparin target: 60–100s
Fibrinogen200–400 mg/dL<100 = DIC risk
D-dimer<0.5 µg/mL↑ = PE/DVT, DIC
Bleeding time1–9 minutes
Thrombin time15–19 sec
💊Anticoagulation Drug Monitoring
DrugMonitorTherapeutic Range
Warfarin (Coumadin)PT/INRINR 2.0–3.0 (most)
Warfarin (mechanical valve)INRINR 2.5–3.5
Heparin (unfractionated)aPTTaPTT 60–100 sec
LMWH (enoxaparin)Anti-Xa (renal)No routine monitoring
Antidote: WarfarinVitamin K (phytonadione)
Antidote: HeparinProtamine sulfate
🫀 Lipid Panel
🫀Lipid Profile
ComponentDesirableBorderline/High Risk
Total Cholesterol<200 mg/dL200–239 = borderline
LDL ("bad")<100 mg/dL130–159 = borderline
LDL (cardiac risk)<70 mg/dL optimal
HDL ("good") male>40 mg/dL<40 = risk factor
HDL ("good") female>50 mg/dL
Triglycerides<150 mg/dL150–199 = borderline
Triglycerides (high)≥500 = pancreatitis risk
🔬 Urinalysis (UA) Normal Values
🔬Urinalysis
ParameterNormalAbnormal Significance
ColorPale to dark yellowRed = blood/myoglobin
ClarityClearCloudy = infection
pH4.5 – 8.0Alkaline = UTI (Proteus)
Specific Gravity1.005 – 1.0301.001 = DI; >1.030 = dehydrated
ProteinNegative (trace OK)+ = nephrotic syndrome, HTN
GlucoseNegative+ = DM, Cushing's
KetonesNegative+ = DKA, starvation
Blood (RBCs)Negative+ = UTI, stones, trauma
WBCs0–5 /HPF>5 = pyuria (UTI)
Casts (hyaline)0–2 /LPFRBC casts = glomerulonephritis
BacteriaNone+ = UTI (with symptoms)
NitritesNegative+ = gram-negative bacteria
Leukocyte esteraseNegative+ = WBCs, UTI
📋 Other Important Lab Values
🦋Thyroid Function
TestNormalSignificance
TSH0.4–4.0 µIU/mLBest screening test
Free T40.8–1.8 ng/dL
Free T32.3–4.2 pg/mL
Hypothyroid patternTSH↑, T4↓
Hyperthyroid patternTSH↓, T4↑
💉Cardiac Markers
MarkerNormalTiming (MI)
Troponin I<0.04 ng/mLRises 3–6h; peaks 12–24h; lasts 7–10d
Troponin T<0.01 ng/mLMost specific for MI
CK-MB<5% of total CKRises 4–6h; peaks 12–24h
Myoglobin<90 ng/mLFirst to rise (2–3h); not specific
BNP<100 pg/mLHeart failure marker
NT-proBNP<300 pg/mLLonger half-life
🧬Other Key Lab Values
TestNormal
Serum Osmolality280–300 mOsm/kg
Urine Osmolality300–900 mOsm/kg
Iron (male)65–175 µg/dL
Iron (female)50–170 µg/dL
Ferritin (male)12–300 ng/mL
Ferritin (female)12–150 ng/mL
TIBC250–370 µg/dL
ESR (male)0–15 mm/hr
ESR (female)0–20 mm/hr
CRP<10 mg/L
Procalcitonin<0.1 ng/mL
Amylase30–110 U/L
Lipase23–300 U/L
Ammonia15–45 µmol/L
Lactate0.5–2.0 mmol/L
🤰OB/Obstetric Key Values
ParameterNormal/Threshold
hCG (non-pregnant)<5 mIU/mL
Fundal height (20–36wks)= weeks of gestation ± 2 cm
Preeclampsia BP≥140/90 after 20 wks
Severe preeclampsia≥160/110
Fetal HR (normal)110–160 bpm
Biophysical Profile (BPP)8–10 (normal) — 2pts each
NST reactive2 accelerations ≥15 bpm × 15 sec in 20 min
APGAR (newborn)7–10 normal (checked 1&5 min)

D/H × V formula

Intake minus Output
Balance = Total Intake − Total Output
IV FluidsmL

Urine OutmL

🔥
Parkland Formula
Burn fluid resuscitation
Total 24h = 4 mL × kg × %TBSA  |  First 8h = ½ total  |  Next 16h = ½ total
Weightkg
%TBSA burned%
0
mL total in 24 hours (Lactated Ringer's)
👧
Pediatric Drug Dose
Clark's Rule & Young's Rule
Clark's: (wt lb ÷ 150) × adult dose  |  Young's: [age ÷ (age+12)] × adult dose
Adult dosemg
Child weightlb
Child ageyrs

💡 Exam Tips
⏱️
Exam Mode gives you 72 seconds per item — the PNLE standard. Use Study Mode for instant feedback with no time pressure.
🎯
Passing score: 75%. Consistently hitting 80%+ in practice means you're well prepared.
🔄
Questions are fully randomized every session — same subject, different set each time.
🚩
Use the flag button on tricky items during the exam so you can review them specifically at the end.
📊
After every session, check Analytics to see which subjects and difficulty levels need the most work.
📄 Export Questions
Subject
Difficulty
Include in Export
Downloads directly to your device as a PDF file

Your current progress will be submitted and you'll see your full results with rationales.
